channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Thousands gather for anti-racism rally in Belfast after disorder
item.description: Protesters gathered at Belfast City Hall for a demonstration against racism, after the past week of violence and disorder.

item.title: Protecting native ash trees from pest that can strip them bare
item.description: Scientists will spend the next three years looking at the best, environmentally-friendly, ways to deal with the pest.

item.title: The night people in Belfast fled their homes because of racist violence
item.description: Reporter Richard Morgan recalls the chaotic scenes he witnessed when reporting on days of disorder in Northern Ireland.

item.title: 'I kept the abuse a secret for half a century - but now I'm ready to talk about it'
item.description: Francis Bostrom speaks for the first time in more than 50 years about abuse he says he suffered as a choirboy in the Church of Ireland.

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney 'back home' on two-day visit to Ireland
item.description: He, and his wife Diana Fox Carney, arrived at Dublin Airport at about 10:00 local time and met Taoiseach (Irish PM) Micheál Martin.

item.title: In pictures: Craic to the future - DeLorean owners celebrate 45-year anniversary
item.description: The Delorean was built in Dunmurry during the 1970s and was designed by American John DeLorean.

item.title: Five arrested after 'sectarian-motivated' stone throwing
item.description: Police are treating the incident as sectarian-motivated.

item.title: Donegal lacked spark in Cork loss - McGuinness
item.description: Donegal boss Jim McGuinness says "there wasn't enough energy" in his side as they let a seven point lead slip to lose 0-17 to 1-13 to Cork in their All-Ireland SFC round two clash in Ballybofey.

item.title: Two local business vans destroyed in Newry arson
item.description: It happened at about 23:25 BST on Friday in North Street in Newry, County Down.

item.title: Is it OK to play AI songs on the radio?
item.description: AI-generated songs are increasingly finding their way onto radio station playlists.

item.title: Scotland beat Ireland to earn first T20 World Cup win
item.description: Scotland secure their first T20 World Cup win thanks to a 40-run victory over Ireland in the Old Trafford opener.

item.title: Prosecutor tells Donaldson he is 'the only one telling lies' at his sex abuse trial
item.description: Sir Jeffrey Donaldson, the former Democratic Unionist Party (DUP) leader, is on trial for 18 sex abuse charges, including one count of rape, all of which he denies.

item.title: Man's arm and hand 'significantly' injured in Lisburn assault
item.description: Police are appealing for information and witnesses after a man in his 30s was assaulted in Lisburn.

item.title: Specialist doctors to join strike in dispute over pay
item.description: It comes after consultant and resident doctors voted in favour on Monday.

item.title: Nurse considering leaving NI 'to save her life'
item.description: A Nigerian nurse who has lived in NI for about five years says she is considering leaving following recent unrest.

item.title: What we know about how Northern Ireland's riots were organised
item.description: Northern Ireland has seen three nights of violence after footage of a knife attack on Monday night spread across social media.

item.title: Belfast knife attack victim's condition improving, says DUP leader
item.description: Gavin Robinson has confirmed that he met the family of Stephen Ogilvie who asked him to share the update.

item.title: Resident left 'scared' after address posted online
item.description: A woman who has been living in NI for a year says she feels unsafe and scared following recent violence.

item.title: Protests pass off without major incident after two nights of unrest
item.description: After two nights of unrest, pockets of protests held across Northern Ireland have passed off without major incident.

item.title: 'I haven't left my house, I'm scared', says Mean Girls actor as Belfast shows cancelled over violence
item.description: The Mean Girls musical's run in Belfast is cancelled following disorder in the city.

item.title: What we know about Belfast attack and disorder
item.description: Houses across the city have been set on fire following a knife attack earlier this week.

item.title: I will never get over watching my home of 13 years burn down
item.description: A night of disorder in Belfast saw people forced to flee their homes, in protests that followed a knife attack in the north of the city.

item.title: What the data does and doesn't tell us about asylum seekers in Northern Ireland
item.description: Belfast police say a refugee charged with the attempted murder of a man on Monday initially entered the country from the Republic of Ireland.

item.title: Government to 'intensify' crackdown on illegal migrants in NI
item.description: According to a UK government source, the Home Office is ramping up immigration enforcement efforts.

item.title: Police under sustained attack in second night of disorder
item.description: Riot police came under sustained attack from a group throwing bricks, bottles and pieces of wood at a major roundabout to the north-west of Belfast.

item.title: Lace tutor among those on King's Honours list
item.description: More than 80 people from Northern Ireland are honoured on the list which celebrates the public service of individuals across the UK.

item.title: 'My son's story should be a warning to every parent'
item.description: A councillor is telling her son’s story to raise awareness following his suicide last year.

item.title: 'I got frostbite climbing Mount Everest - now I'm back treating my patients'
item.description: Reaching the top of Mount Everest is a feat that relatively few Irish climbers have achieved, and Sarah Armstrong, from Crossmaglen, did just that.

item.title: I wrote a poem about my son - years later it appeared on his exam paper
item.description: Emily Cullen says she couldn't believe it when her son told her about the poem, which she had written seven years earlier.

item.title: BBC Newsline Signed Summary
item.description: A signed summary of BBC Newsline.

item.title: Climbing the ladder - the drive to recruit more women firefighters
item.description: Come-and-try days, specifically for women, hope to encourage more to consider a career on the front line.

item.title: Should smacking children be illegal? Proposal to change law dropped
item.description: Northern Ireland is one of two places in the UK and Ireland to not have outlawed smacking children.

item.title: From rappers to rock: Everything you need to know about Belsonic
item.description: All you need to know about the annual Belfast festival as it gears up for 10 nights of music over three weeks in Ormeau Park.

item.title: Meet the park poet helping strangers unlock their inner thoughts
item.description: Student Patrick Kruse has written dozen of poems with strangers in Belfast as a way to help people unlock their most vulnerable self.

item.title: Love Island's cracking on! So what is it actually like in the villa?
item.description: BBC News NI speaks to former Love Islander Matthew MacNabb as series 13 cracks on.

item.title: From pub challenge to growing sport, arm wrestling catches families in its grip
item.description: Arm wrestling is becoming a competitive sport, with families driving a rise in participation.

item.title: 'I never expected to sing it to a dog!', Tim Wheeler
item.description: Wheeler is the singer and guitarist for the band Ash, which was formed in Downpatrick in County Down in 1992.

item.title: Feuding within Ulster Unionists 'would make an Eastenders' scriptwriter blush'
item.description: In the long, inglorious history of political self-destruction the Ulster Unionist Party record takes some beating.

item.title: How Taylor finally landed dream Croke Park swansong
item.description: Katie Taylor says "I'm definitely going to have to hold back the tears" when she walks out for her final professional fight at Croke Park in Dublin on 5 September.

item.title: Kate Winslet delights young film fan with surprise call
item.description: Teenager Eryn, who has been blind since brith, made a three hour round-trip for a film screening because there was no local accessible cinema.

item.title: 'It's a very big deal' - curlew eggs hatch after being saved from wildfire
item.description: The four chicks, which hatched from the eggs, are being cared for until they are old and strong enough to be released.

item.title: Green space v parking: City divided in row over seafront project
item.description: The developer has revised the plans for the first phase to include additional parking and a bigger play park.

item.title: 'Every minute counts when it comes to new ovarian cancer treatment'
item.description: The new drug offers ovarian cancer patients more time and a better quality of life.

item.title: How a content creator tried to use a YouTube alibi to get away with murder
item.description: Stephen McCullagh, 36, of Woodland Gardens in Lisburn, will serve at least 31 years in prison for the murder of his pregnant partner Natalie McNally.

item.title: Police investigate sex assault on two teenage girls
item.description: Police have described the suspect in the alleged assault in Londonderry as a white male.

item.title: Man who kicked and dragged dog across car park sentenced
item.description: The judge said Oran Mitchell, from Londonderry, had committed an "appalling act".

item.title: Connolly appointed Derry City director of football
item.description: Mark Connolly is appointed Derry City director of football after his decision to call time on his playing career.

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for courthouse attack accused
item.description: Corie McKenna, 34, failed to attend a hearing at Strabane Magistrates' Court on Wednesday.

item.title: BBC iPlayer to stream Derry City's home games
item.description: Derry City's remaining home games in the 2026 League of Ireland Premier Division season will be streamed live on BBC iPlayer.

item.title: Man convicted of terrorism offences over PSNI data breach
item.description: Christopher Paul O'Kane, 53, denied the charges under the Terrorism Act linked to a PSNI data breach.

item.title: Police treat fire at derelict building as arson
item.description: Firefighters took more than three hours to deal with the blaze in the Maydown area of Derry on Sunday.

item.title: Mum of three 'petrified' during suspected petrol bomb attack
item.description: A mother of three praises her 14-year-old son's "incredible bravery" in putting out the flames.

item.title: Bathing warning lifted after pollution investigation
item.description: Officials say the water at Portstewart Strand does not pose a risk to the public.

item.title: Bathing warning issued after 'potential water pollution incident'
item.description: The National Trust, which manages Portstewart Strand, has urged visitors to the beach to be vigilant.

item.title: Needles linked to drug use found in car park
item.description: The items were taken away for disposal after being found in Limavady, County Londonderry, on Tuesday.

item.title: Councillor given one-month suspension over social media post
item.description: BBC News NI understands the post included a reference to the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI).

item.title: Derry and Strabane records major tourism growth in 2025
item.description: Figures from the NI Statistics and Research Agency (Nisra) represent a major year-on-year boost for the local tourism sector,

item.title: 'I started working here at 15 - now I'm retiring after 75 years'
item.description: Sadie Jefferson started work at the pharmacy, aged 15, back in 1951 and has worked there for 75 years.

item.title: Plans for gun licence price hike withdrawn
item.description: Justice minister Naomi Long ended a consultation seeking views on plans to increase the cost of a gun license on Wednesday.

item.title: Anger after needles found near children's play area
item.description: The used syringes were discarded in the village of Ballykelly, County Londonderry.

item.title: Republic of Ireland v Israel to be played at neutral venue
item.description: The Football Association of Ireland confirms the Republic of Ireland's home Nations League fixture with Israel later this year will be held at a neutral venue.

item.title: Senior member of Kinahan crime group sentenced to 24 years in prison
item.description: Sean McGovern will serve two sentences of directing the activities of a criminal organisation.

item.title: Further infant remains uncovered at former mother-and-baby home
item.description: Experts who are carrying out the dig at Tuam in County Galway have published their latest regular update.

item.title: Ireland issues travel bans for two Israeli ministers
item.description: The bans for Itamar Ben-Gvir and Bezalel Smotrich are confirmed by the Taoiseach (Irish prime minister) Micheál Martin.

item.title: Taylor to face Pili in Croke Park farewell
item.description: Katie Taylor will bring the curtain down on her decorated career in a farewell fight against Flora Pili at Croke Park.

item.title: Ireland facing EU court case over peat extraction
item.description: The European Commission accuses Irish authorities of failing to properly enforce EU regulations.

item.title: Ferry search leads to €2.9m drugs seizure
item.description: A man in his 20s is arrested after revenue staff search a freight unit which travelled from France.

item.title: Body of man found after fishing boat sinks
item.description: Another person was rescued by a local boat during the search operation in Carlingford Lough.

item.title: Man given life sentence for murdering his mother
item.description: Annie Heyneman was murdered at her home in County Cavan in January 2025

item.title: Holocaust survivor Tomi Reichental dies aged 90
item.description: Tomi Reichental, who lost 35 close family members in the Holocaust, moved to the Republic of Ireland in 1959.

item.title: Man dead after assault in Dublin
item.description: The fatal assault happened on Clarendon Street in the early hours of Monday morning.

item.title: Three deaths on Irish roads over bank holiday weekend
item.description: The separate crashes occurred in County Louth, Roscommon and Dublin.

item.title: Man charged with murder of woman in County Galway
item.description: The victim, who was in her 30s, sustained slash and stab wounds in an attack earlier this week.

item.title: In Case You Missed It
item.description: You may know them as a supermarket chain but in Northern Ireland Lidl is opening a pub.
